title: asclepias simplex
intro: Asclepias simplex, also known as the white milkweed or antelope sensitive plant, is a perennial plant species native to North America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include the ability to induce vomiting, reduce fever, and treat various skin conditions due to its high content of cardenolides and glycosides. The health benefits of Asclepias simplex include its potential to treat conditions such as eczema, dermatitis, and snake bites, as well as its ability to act as a natural anti-inflammatory and antiseptic. The plant can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
